definitions.definition: the Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority established under section 4
the granting of a contract by a procuring and disposing entity to a supplier for purposes of entering into a procurement contract

an offer to provide goods , works and services submitted by a bidder in response to an invitation from a procuring and disposing entity and includes a tender, proposal, quotation and, where applicable, an application to pre qualify
any participant who has expressed interest in procurement proceedings by submitting a bid
a tender solicitation document or any other document for solicitation of offers, proposals or quotations

any advertisement by which eligible bidders are invited to submit written offers to provide or acquire works , services and goods or any combination of them in case of procurement and disposal respectively
a security bond required to ensure that a bid will remain valid during the period stated in the bidding document
a declaration specified under the regulations
the chairperson of the Authority
a spouse, child, sibling or parent or guardian
the head or principal person who is in charge of procuring and disposing entity
a person who is under a procurement contract to provide consultancy services to a procuring and disposing entity
services of an intellectual or advisory nature
a legally binding agreement between two or more parties formed by the mutual consent of the parties
a natural or legal person who is registered with a relevant authority to perform construction works
ascribed to the term by the Corrupt Practices Act
the Director General appointed under section 10

the divestiture of public assets, including intellectual and proprietary rights and goodwill, and any other rights of a procuring and disposing entity by any means of sale, auction or any combination of these
the successive stages in the disposal cycle
any bidder whose principal place of business is within Malaŵi
any supplier whose principal place of business is within Malaŵi
an organization which provides, or joins in providing, grants, credits or loans to the Government or its agencies
the assessment of a bidder ’s or supplier ’s technical, financial and management capabilities and the bidder ’s or supplier ’s governance record to effectively execute a procurement contract
a situation which poses an imminent threat to the physical safety of a population or damage to property
the process of procurement using the internet or other information and communication technologies
a bidder other than a domestic or regional bidder
a supplier other than a domestic or regional supplier

an agreement between a procuring and a disposing entity and a supplier and a contractor through which a supplier or contractor undertakes to supply goods , works or services on indefinite quantity basis at an agreed unit price for an agreed period of time
a misrepresentation of facts in order to influence a procurement or disposal process or the execution of a contract

objects of every kind and description, including raw materials, products and equipment, and objects in solid, liquid or gaseous form, and electricity, as well as services incidental to the supply of the goods if the value of those incidental services does not exceed the value of the goods themselves
written, visual, oral and electronic information
a committee constituted under section 26
an association of two or more bidders for the purposes of participating in and carrying out a procurement contract
assigned to it in the Local Government Act
supplier , contractor or consulting firm registered and operating in Malaŵi and whose shareholding is held in majority by Malaŵian nationals
a bidder ranked as first, following the application of the specified evaluation methodology and criteria
a procurement that has not followed the laid down procedures and guidelines
a supplier registered and operating in Malaŵi
any object of procurement other than goods , works and consulting services
permission to proceed with the intention for contract award
a person who is assigned to, or employed by, the Authority
the conduct of due diligence to determine whether the bidder is qualified to perform the procurement contract effectively
documents issued by the procuring and disposing entity that set out the terms and conditions of the pre-qualification proceedings

an agreement between a procuring and disposing entity and a bidder , supplier , or consultant resulting from the procurement proceedings and includes a framework agreement or an agreement to dispose public assets, if applicable in the context
a member of the cadre of procurement professionals
a division or section in each procuring and disposing entity responsible for the execution of the procurement and disposal function
a Government ministry, department, agency, any other public body or any sub division thereof engaging in procurement or disposal of public assets
something that is to the advantage, direct or indirect, of the people of Malaŵi
acquisition by a procuring and disposing entity , of goods , works , services or any combination thereof, funded in whole or in part by public funds
any property, tangible or intangible, owned by the Government or by a procuring and disposing entity , including physical property, shares, proprietary rights and land
any organ or agency of the Government and includes— (a) a statutory body

any monetary resources appropriated to procuring and disposing entities through budgetary processes, grants and credits, put at the disposal of procuring and disposing entities by donor organizations or revenues of procuring and disposal entities
any recorded information , in any format, including an electronic format
a bidder from countries in the region or regional block as prescribed in the regulations
the margin of preference applicable to bidders and suppliers from the countries in the region or regional block, as stipulated in the bidding document
satisfying the minimum requirements of a bidding process as stipulated in the bidding document

an online real-time sourcing technique utilized by a procuring and disposing entity to select the successful bid , which involves presentation by suppliers or contractors of successively lowered bids during a scheduled period of time and automated evaluation of the bids
the independent Review Committee constituted under Part IX
environmental, social, economic and other policies of the Government which a procuring and disposing entity is required to take into account in the procurement and disposal proceedings

an enterprise including its affiliates that is independently owned and operated, not dominant in the field of operation in which it is bidding on Government contracts and qualified as such under the criteria and size standards in the relevant instruments of the Ministry responsible for industry and trade or the National Construction Industry Council of Malaŵi
assigned to it in the Public Audit Act
a natural, or legal person, who has entered into a procurement contract with a procuring and disposing entity
pressure or influence exerted on a public officer to make a decision in favour of the person exerting such pressure or influence or anyone connected to him

any department, division, branch or section of the procuring and disposing entity , including any project unit working under the authority of the procuring and disposing entity , which initiates procurement and disposal requirements and is the user of the requirements
the person appointed as vice-chairperson of the Authority

works associated with the construction, re-construction, demolition, repair or renovation of a building, road, structure or works, such as site preparation, excavation, erection, building, installation of equipment or materials, decoration and finishing, as well as services incidental to construction such as drilling, mapping, satellite photography, seismic investigations, and related services provided pursuant to a procurement contract , if the value of those services does not exceed that of the works themselves.
